slides.title: Your outlook is a *health behavior*.
slides.sub: Optimism is linked to a longer, healthier life. The good news: it can be practiced.

slides.title: Optimism and a long life
slides.body: In studies, a more optimistic outlook *is linked to* living longer and staying healthier into later years.

slides.title: Not toxic positivity
slides.body: This isn't ignoring problems. It is *expecting good*, and acting on it, while still facing what is real.

slides.title: Better habits, less stress
slides.body: Optimism tends to support *healthier daily habits* and lower stress, two things that quietly shape how you age.

slides.title: You can practice it
slides.body: Gratitude and reframing are simple tools. Naming *one good thing* daily can slowly shift your default view.

slides.title: The future shapes today
slides.body: How you see tomorrow shapes what you do now. A hopeful view *quietly nudges* better choices each day.

slides.title: Let a hopeful view *add up*.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the sleep, food, movement and steps behind a good day, all in one calm daily number.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your outlook isn't just a mood. Optimism is linked to a longer, healthier life. 🌤️

It isn't toxic positivity, it's expecting good and acting on it. It supports better habits and lower stress, and you can practice it with gratitude and reframing. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the habits behind a good day into one calm score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Optimism Is Linked to a Longer Life
